proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one of the 77 binary (0/1) features differs in objective_response rate between its two levels (univariate screen vs objective_response).

analyses.result_summary: Univariate chi-square screen of all 77 binary features against objective_response. Multiple features show p<0.05; the smallest p-values are feature_013 (p=1.1e-33), feature_067 (p=2.0e-18), feature_006 (p=1.0e-6), feature_007 (p=5.9e-4) and feature_039 (p=3.4e-3).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_013=1 have a LOWER objective_response rate than those with feature_013=0.
Patients with feature_067=1 have a LOWER objective_response rate than those with feature_067=0.
Patients with feature_006=1 have a HIGHER objective_response rate than those with feature_006=0.
Patients with feature_007=1 have a HIGHER objective_response rate than those with feature_007=0.
Patients with feature_039=1 have a HIGHER objective_response rate than those with feature_039=0.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate 15.4% with feature_013=1 (n=32365) vs 19.6% with feature_013=0 (n=17635). OR=0.74, chi-square p=1.1e-33. Direction confirmed (negative effect).
Response rate 14.4% with feature_067=1 (n=12584) vs 17.7% with feature_067=0. OR=0.78, p=2.0e-18. Direction confirmed (negative effect).
Response rate 18.2% with feature_006=1 (n=14258) vs 16.4% with feature_006=0. OR=1.14, p=1.0e-6. Direction confirmed (positive effect).
Response rate 17.5% with feature_007=1 (n=22371) vs 16.4% with feature_007=0. OR=1.09, p=5.9e-4. Direction confirmed (positive effect).
Response rate 17.4% with feature_039=1 (n=25008) vs 16.4% with feature_039=0. OR=1.07, p=3.4e-3. Direction confirmed (positive effect).

proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one continuous feature has a different mean between objective_response=1 and objective_response=0 (univariate continuous screen).

analyses.result_summary: Welch t-tests of all 35 continuous features. Smallest p-values: feature_011 (p=2.8e-35), feature_099 (p=1.3e-4), feature_063 (p=4.2e-4), feature_092 (p=3.8e-3). Most other continuous features (age=feature_078, labs) show no univariate association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_011 values are associated with LOWER objective_response (negative slope on the response probability).
Higher feature_099 values are associated with HIGHER objective_response (positive slope, biologically consistent with serum albumin).
Higher feature_063 values are associated with LOWER objective_response (negative slope, biologically consistent with an inflammatory or tumor-burden marker).
Higher feature_092 values are associated with HIGHER objective_response (positive slope, biologically consistent with a predictive biomarker such as PD-L1).

analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_011: 3.38 in responders vs 3.93 in non-responders (diff=-0.55, t=-12.4, p=2.8e-35). Direction confirmed (negative).
Mean feature_099: 3.821 in responders vs 3.798 in non-responders (diff=+0.023, t=+3.82, p=1.3e-4). Direction confirmed (positive).
Mean feature_063: 5.78 in responders vs 6.15 in non-responders (diff=-0.37, t=-3.53, p=4.2e-4). Direction confirmed (negative).
Mean feature_092: 0.313 in responders vs 0.306 in non-responders (diff=+0.0075, t=+2.90, p=3.8e-3). Direction confirmed (small positive main effect).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across the ordinal feature_051 (levels 0/1/2, plausibly performance status), HIGHER values are associated with monotonically LOWER objective_response (negative trend).
Other ordinal features (feature_026, feature_033, feature_064, feature_018, feature_045, feature_042) also show monotonic trends in objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Response rates by feature_051 level: 21.1% (level 0, n=17557), 15.7% (level 1, n=24962), 11.1% (level 2, n=7481). Strong monotonic decrease. Chi-square p=2.9e-93; point-biserial r=-0.092, p=7.9e-95. Direction confirmed (large negative trend).

No monotonic trend reaches p<0.05 in chi-square: feature_026 p=0.10, feature_033 p=0.21, feature_064 p=0.82, feature_018 p=0.64, feature_045 p=0.19, feature_042 p=0.91. Hypothesis NOT supported for these other ordinal features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Objective response rate differs across histology categories (feature_043 = adenocarcinoma vs squamous).
Objective response rate differs across smoking status (feature_057 = current/former/never).
Objective response rate differs across self-reported race (feature_123).
Objective response rate differs across insurance categories (feature_005).

analyses.result_summary: Response 16.7% adenocarcinoma vs 17.4% squamous (diff=+0.007). Chi-square p=0.078. Not significant on the conventional 0.05 cut-off. Hypothesis NOT supported.
Response 17.2% current vs 16.8% former vs 16.4% never. Chi-square p=0.27. Hypothesis NOT supported.
Response rates: white 16.5%, hispanic 17.2%, asian 17.7%, black 17.8%, other 18.5%. Chi-square p=0.045. Borderline significant; non-white groups trend higher.
Response rates: medicaid 16.1%, medicare 16.8%, private 17.2%, uninsured 17.0%. Chi-square p=0.23. Hypothesis NOT supported overall.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic regression of objective_response on the strongest screened predictors plus demographics, feature_013, feature_067, feature_011 and feature_063 retain SIGNED-NEGATIVE effects, while feature_006, feature_007, feature_039, feature_099 and feature_092 retain SIGNED-POSITIVE effects, and feature_051 retains its monotonic negative gradient.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_013 + feature_067 + feature_006 + feature_007 + feature_039 + feature_011 + feature_099 + feature_063 + feature_092 + C(feature_051) + feature_078 + C(feature_043) + C(feature_057) + C(feature_123) + C(feature_005)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: All hypothesised directions confirmed. Adjusted log-odds coefficients (all p<0.005 unless noted): feature_013 -0.302, feature_067 -0.259, feature_006 +0.131, feature_007 +0.081, feature_039 +0.077, feature_011 -0.0394, feature_099 +0.093, feature_063 -0.0051, feature_092 +0.173, feature_051[T.1] -0.369, feature_051[T.2] -0.777. Age (feature_078) p=0.78; histology, smoking, race not significant after adjustment; private insurance vs medicaid +0.089 (p=0.019). Pseudo-R^2=0.020, LLR p=8e-179.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The combination of feature_006=1 and feature_039=1 is more than additively associated with objective_response (positive multiplicative interaction).
The combination of feature_007=1 and feature_039=1 is more than additively associated with objective_response (positive multiplicative interaction).
The combination of feature_006=1 and feature_007=1 is more than additively associated with objective_response (positive multiplicative interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction term coefficient +0.184, p=4.2e-4. Direction confirmed (positive synergy). Stratified rates: 006=0/039=0 16.3%, 006=0/039=1 16.5%, 006=1/039=0 16.7%, 006=1/039=1 19.7%.
Interaction term coefficient +0.146, p=2.4e-3. Direction confirmed (positive synergy). Stratified rates: 007=0/039=0 16.4%, 007=0/039=1 16.4%, 007=1/039=0 16.5%, 007=1/039=1 18.6%.
Interaction term coefficient +0.149, p=4.3e-3. Direction confirmed (positive synergy). Stratified rates: 006=0/007=0 16.1%, 006=0/007=1 16.7%, 006=1/007=0 16.9%, 006=1/007=1 19.7%.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006, feature_007 and feature_039 act as a TRIPLE COMBINATION: the three-way interaction term feature_006:feature_007:feature_039 is positive and significant in a saturated logistic model, with the only large incremental response benefit appearing when ALL three are present.

analyses.result_summary: Three-way interaction feature_006:feature_007:feature_039 coefficient +0.392, z=3.74, p=1.8e-4. None of the lower-order main or two-way terms are significant in the saturated model, and the cell with all three=1 has 23.0% response (n=3216) versus ~16-17% in every other cell. Direction confirmed (positive triple synergy).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The continuous feature_092 modifies the effect of feature_007 on objective_response (positive feature_092 x feature_007 interaction).
The continuous feature_092 also modifies the effect of feature_006 on objective_response (positive interaction).
The continuous feature_092 also modifies the effect of feature_039 on objective_response (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_092:feature_007 coefficient +0.606, p=7.9e-8. Main effect of feature_092 alone is -0.117 (NS, p=0.13); main effect of feature_007 alone is -0.107 (p=0.012). Net feature_092 slope when feature_007=1 is +0.49. Direction confirmed.
Interaction feature_092:feature_006 coefficient +0.594, p=1.2e-6. Direction confirmed.
Interaction feature_092:feature_039 coefficient +0.591, p=1.6e-7. Direction confirmed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the feature_007=1 stratum, higher feature_092 is associated with HIGHER response, but within the feature_007=0 stratum, feature_092 has no association with response (consistent with feature_092 being a treatment-predictive biomarker for the agent represented by feature_007).

analyses.code: for v in [0,1]: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_092', data=df[df.feature_007==v]).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_007=0 (n=27629): feature_092 coefficient -0.117, p=0.13 (no association). feature_007=1 (n=22371): feature_092 coefficient +0.489, p=3.1e-9. Stratified pattern matches a treatment-predictive biomarker. Direction confirmed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The triple-combination synergy (feature_006=feature_007=feature_039=1) is concentrated in patients with HIGH feature_092: response rate in the top quartile of feature_092 among triple-combo patients is markedly higher than in the bottom three quartiles, while no such gradient exists in patients without the triple combo.

analyses.result_summary: Among patients without the triple combo (n=46784): response rates are 16.7%/16.6%/16.4%/16.2% across feature_092 quartiles (flat). Among patients WITH the triple combo (n=3216): response rates are 16.9%/16.1%/18.2%/40.1% across quartiles. The Q4-triple cell (n=827) has 40% response — by far the highest in the dataset. Direction confirmed; biomarker-treatment interaction is essentially confined to the triple combo.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effects of the major treatment-like features (feature_006, feature_007, feature_039, feature_013, feature_067) on objective_response differ between adenocarcinoma and squamous histology (significant interactions with feature_043).

analyses.result_summary: No interaction with histology approached significance. Smallest interaction p-values: feature_013 x squamous p=0.40, feature_099 x squamous p=0.41. Hypothesis NOT supported — treatment effects appear consistent across histology.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_092 on objective_response differs by smoking status (feature_057), with a smaller or negative slope in NEVER smokers compared to ever smokers.
The effects of the binary 'treatment' features (feature_006, feature_007, feature_013, feature_067) on objective_response differ by smoking status.

analyses.result_summary: feature_092 x smoking[never] interaction coefficient -0.525, p=3.5e-3 (smoking[former] interaction NS). The positive slope of feature_092 on response is attenuated/reversed in never smokers. Direction confirmed.
No binary treatment x smoking interaction reached significance (smallest p among binary-treatment interactions: feature_007 x never p=0.23). Hypothesis NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_007 on objective_response is larger (more positive) in patients with WORSE performance status (feature_051=2) than in those with feature_051=0.
Other binary treatment effects (feature_006, feature_039, feature_013, feature_067) are also modified by performance status.

analyses.result_summary: feature_007 x feature_051[T.2] interaction coefficient +0.179, p=0.030. Modest positive interaction: the response advantage of feature_007=1 is somewhat larger in feature_051=2 patients than in feature_051=0. Direction confirmed.
No other binary-treatment x feature_051 interaction reached significance (smallest p=0.22 for feature_013 x feature_051[T.2]). Hypothesis NOT supported for the other binary features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Age (feature_078) modifies the effect of at least one treatment-like binary feature on objective_response (e.g. feature_067 x feature_078).

analyses.result_summary: Only feature_067 x age reached p<0.05: coefficient +0.0071, p=0.014, indicating the negative effect of feature_067 attenuates with increasing age. All other interactions p>0.19. Direction partially confirmed (one of 9 tested).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Insurance status (feature_005) modifies the effect of any treatment-like binary feature (feature_006, feature_007, feature_013, feature_039, feature_067) on objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: No insurance x treatment interaction term reached p<0.05 (smallest p=0.16 for feature_007 x medicare). Hypothesis NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for clinical factors (feature_051, feature_013, feature_011, feature_099, feature_092), self-reported race (feature_123) is associated with objective_response, with non-white groups having HIGHER adjusted odds than white patients.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ C(feature_123, Treatment("white")) + C(feature_051) + feature_013 + feature_011 + feature_099 + feature_092', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted log-odds vs white: black +0.079 (p=0.034, significant); asian +0.080 (p=0.11); hispanic +0.038 (p=0.28); other +0.130 (p=0.12). Direction confirmed for black; other non-white categories trend positive but NS individually. The unadjusted disparity (slightly lower response in white patients) survives clinical adjustment for the black subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for clinical factors, insurance status (feature_005) is associated with objective_response, with privately insured patients having HIGHER adjusted odds than medicaid patients.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ C(feature_005, Treatment("medicaid")) + C(feature_051) + feature_013 + feature_011 + feature_099 + feature_092', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted log-odds vs medicaid: medicare +0.062 (p=0.099), private +0.087 (p=0.022, significant), uninsured +0.079 (p=0.25). Direction confirmed for private vs medicaid; medicaid patients have the lowest adjusted response odds.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Receipt of the triple combination (feature_006=feature_007=feature_039=1) varies by race (feature_123) — i.e., there is a treatment-allocation disparity that could explain part of the response disparity.
Receipt of the triple combination varies by insurance (feature_005).

analyses.result_summary: Triple-combo receipt by race: white 6.5%, asian 6.6%, hispanic 6.4%, black 6.2%, other 5.8%. Chi-square p=0.83. Hypothesis NOT supported — no race-based disparity in receipt of the triple combo.
Triple-combo receipt by insurance: medicaid 6.6%, medicare 6.7%, private 6.1%, uninsured 6.5%. Chi-square p=0.097. Hypothesis NOT supported at the 0.05 cut-off.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_013 on objective_response is consistent across performance-status strata (no qualitative interaction with feature_051), so feature_013 is not just a proxy for poor performance status.

analyses.result_summary: Stratified rates (feature_013=1 vs feature_013=0): ECOG=0 19.4% vs 24.2% (diff -4.8pp); ECOG=1 14.0% vs 18.7% (diff -4.7pp); ECOG=2 10.4% vs 12.2% (diff -1.8pp). The negative association is present in every performance-status stratum, with somewhat reduced absolute magnitude at ECOG=2. Direction confirmed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The four-way interaction feature_013:feature_006:feature_007:feature_039 is significant (i.e., feature_013 modifies the strength of the triple-combo synergy).

analyses.result_summary: Four-way interaction term coefficient +0.170, p=0.43 (NOT significant). The three-way feature_006:feature_007:feature_039 synergy coefficient remains large (+0.29, p=0.080 in this saturated specification). feature_013 has independent main and 2-way effects but does not modulate the triple synergy. Hypothesis NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_011 on objective_response is monotonically negative across deciles, consistent with a count-like adverse prognostic variable (e.g. number of metastatic sites, prior lines).
feature_011 interacts with the triple combo (the triple-combo benefit is smaller in patients with high feature_011).

analyses.result_summary: Response rate decreases nearly monotonically across feature_011 octiles: 19.2%, 18.2%, 16.7%, 17.2%, 15.9%, 15.7%, 14.3%, 13.2%. Direction confirmed (negative monotonic gradient).
feature_011:triple interaction coefficient +0.0042, p=0.72 (NS). Both feature_011 (-0.039) and triple (+0.402) act independently. Hypothesis NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A REFINED multivariable logistic model containing the main effects of feature_013, feature_067, feature_011, feature_099, feature_063, feature_051 plus the saturated feature_006*feature_007*feature_039 set and the feature_092*feature_007 interaction fits significantly better than the same model without interactions (LRT p<0.05) and reproduces all the previously identified directions.

analyses.code: m1 = smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_013 + feature_067 + feature_011 + feature_099 + feature_063 + C(feature_051) + feature_006*feature_007*feature_039 + feature_092*feature_007', data=df).fit(); m0 = smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_013 + feature_067 + feature_011 + feature_099 + feature_063 + C(feature_051) + feature_006 + feature_007 + feature_039 + feature_092', data=df).fit(); 2*(m1.llf - m0.llf)
analyses.result_summary: LRT chi-square=73.1 on 5 df, p=2.3e-14. Refined model significantly improves on the no-interaction model. In the refined model the triple feature_006:feature_007:feature_039 coefficient is +0.415 (p=8.5e-5) and the feature_092:feature_007 coefficient is +0.611 (p=8.5e-8); all other prior signs (feature_013 -0.30, feature_067 -0.26, feature_011 -0.040, feature_099 +0.094, feature_063 -0.005, feature_051 strong negative gradient) are reproduced. Direction confirmed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Combining the dataset's strongest signals: the highest objective_response rate in the cohort is observed in the patient subgroup defined by feature_051=0, feature_013=0, triple combo (feature_006=feature_007=feature_039=1), and top-quartile feature_092 — substantially above the cohort baseline of 16.9%.

analyses.result_summary: Subgroup feature_051=0 & feature_013=0 & triple combo & top-quartile feature_092: n=104, response rate 42.3%. Across feature_092 quartiles within this otherwise-favorable subgroup the response rates are Q1 19.2%, Q2 18.8%, Q3 30.3%, Q4 42.3% — a steep biomarker gradient ONLY visible inside the triple-combo subgroup. This is the highest-responding subgroup identified in the cohort and is roughly 2.5x the baseline of 16.9%. Direction confirmed.
